query.original: Does a Barbarian lose their Rage bonus damage if they cast a spell while raging?
query.used: Does a Barbarian lose their Rage bonus damage if they cast a spell while raging?

retrieved_nodes.preview:
While raging, you gain the following benefits if you aren't wearing heavy armor:

- You have advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.
If you are able to cast spells, you can't cast them or concentrate on them while raging.
- When you make a melee weapon attack using Strength, you gain a bonus to the damage roll that increases as you gain levels as a barbarian, as shown in the Rage Damage column of the Barbarian table.
If you are charmed or frightened when you enter your rage, the effect is suspended for the duration of the rage.
You can also end your rage on your turn as a bonus action.
If you do so, for the duration of your rage you can make a single melee weapon attack as a bonus action on each of your turns after this one.
On your turn, you can enter a rage as a bonus action.

If the creature can see or hear you, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn.

Additionally, if you are surprised at the beginning of combat and aren't incapacitated, you can act normally on your first turn, but only if you enter your rage before doing anything else on that turn

### Relentless Rage

Starting at 11th level, your rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds.

answer: Yes, according to the provided rules, a Barbarian cannot cast spells or concentrate on them while raging, so they would also lose their Rage bonus damage.

correctness.feedback: The generated answer correctly states that a Barbarian cannot cast spells while raging, which aligns with the reference answer. However, it inaccurately implies that the Barbarian would lose their Rage bonus damage due to casting spells, which is misleading since they cannot cast spells at all while raging.
